evidence_quotes.quote: video games they have already sold
deliberately prevent customers from retaining their purchase.

paragraphs.text: In this new law there should be a clause, about publishers permanently disabling video games they have already sold ie some publishers are selling video games under terms, that may violate 93/13/EEC and deliberately prevent customers from retaining their purchase. I wouldn't want to lose the games i have already paid for and spent lots and lots of my hard earned money on. I don't want a future where "you will own nothing and be happy" is the norm. We, as consumers, should be protected from predatory and/or greedy tactics of the industry (either video game developers or publishers). We should have full rights to hold the products we already obtained. The industry should not have absolutely any rights to take them arbitrarily from us, just because they feel like it.
